purpose: Bounded review of whether existing OECD GDP unit-basis evidence supports mapping status advancement, separate provisional treatments, or continued deferral.

scope.source_evidence: existing TASK-038, TASK-039, and TASK-040 artifacts only

governing_doctrine.constraint: High-impact GDP mappings require review; confidence or provider-code match is routing metadata, not truth.
OECD B1GQ remained deferred because USD_EXC and USD_PPP unit-basis caveats were material and report integration was deferred.
Advancement requires a review-approved unit-basis policy distinguishing exchange-rate USD and PPP USD and documenting downstream report eligibility.
Existing evidence now separates USD_EXC and USD_PPP basis candidates but does not approve accepted/provisional mapping status.

overall_conclusion.summary: TASK-040 satisfies the evidentiary precondition that OECD USD_EXC and USD_PPP must be separated. It does not, by itself, supply the review-approved basis/report policy required to advance either candidate to governed provisional status. Both candidates should remain deferred today, while being treated as distinct future provisional-treatment candidates rather than one combined OECD GDP mapping.
overall_conclusion.reason_not_approved: No explicit review decision has selected exchange-rate-vs-PPP policy, report eligibility semantics, caveat representation in accepted/provisional state, or comparability relationship to WDI/current-USD outputs.

comparability_implications: USD_EXC is an exchange-rate current-USD basis and must not be merged with PPP-basis USD evidence.
It may be closer to WDI current-USD metadata than USD_PPP, but current evidence still does not prove report-safe comparability without an explicit policy and caveat model.
It should be evaluated as a basis-specific candidate, not as generic OECD GDP truth.

report_eligibility.reason: Report integration remains deferred; no accepted report policy says exchange-rate OECD values can enter comparable GDP outputs alongside WDI or PPP-basis values.
report_eligibility.minimum_future_condition: A review decision must define exchange-rate current-USD report semantics, required caveats, and whether outputs are same-basis comparable, side-by-side only, or excluded.

remaining_uncertainty: Whether current exchange-rate USD is acceptable as a provisional comparator to WDI current-USD metadata.
Whether OECD USD_EXC scale/metadata and coverage are sufficiently explicit for provisional report use.
How accepted/provisional state should encode basis caveats and report exclusion/eligibility.
minimum_future_evidence_required: Review-approved exchange-rate basis policy for OECD B1GQ.
Evidence link showing USD_EXC unit metadata source and stable interpretation.
Explicit report-impact decision preserving no-conversion/no-aggregation boundaries.
State/manifest deltas only after review approval; no direct base-state mutation.

comparability_implications: USD_PPP is a PPP-basis current-USD profile and must not be compared as if it were exchange-rate current USD.
PPP-basis GDP may be useful for separate analytical views, but it requires a separate report treatment or canonical comparability profile.
Combining USD_PPP with USD_EXC/WDI current-USD outputs without labeling would create misleading cross-country/time comparisons.

report_eligibility.reason: Report integration remains deferred and PPP-basis semantics differ materially from exchange-rate current-USD semantics.
report_eligibility.minimum_future_condition: A review decision must define a PPP-specific report treatment or explicitly exclude PPP values from current-USD exchange-rate GDP outputs.

remaining_uncertainty: Whether MacroForge wants a separate PPP GDP canonical/report profile at this stage.
Whether PPP-basis evidence should become a distinct provisional treatment or remain excluded until a PPP-specific report exists.
How downstream reports should prevent accidental mixing of PPP and exchange-rate basis values.
minimum_future_evidence_required: Review-approved PPP basis policy for OECD B1GQ.
Evidence link showing USD_PPP unit metadata source and stable interpretation.
Explicit decision on separate PPP report eligibility versus continued exclusion.
State/manifest deltas only after review approval; no direct base-state mutation.

comparability_implications: The existing evidence supports separating comparability profiles, not collapsing them into one OECD GDP mapping.
Exchange-rate USD and PPP USD must remain disjoint comparability groups unless a later policy explicitly models their relationship.
Basis distinction reduces ambiguity but does not resolve semantic acceptance for high-impact GDP mappings.
A future governed provisional path is plausible for each basis only as separate treatments with explicit caveats.
report_eligibility_implications: No OECD B1GQ basis candidate is currently eligible for comparable GDP report integration from this review alone.
USD_EXC could become eligible for an exchange-rate/current-USD report only after a review-approved policy and explicit caveat/state representation.
USD_PPP should require either a PPP-specific report/profile or explicit exclusion from exchange-rate GDP reports.
Existing GDP reports should remain unchanged.
future_advancement_conditions: Record a review decision that selects separate basis-specific treatment rather than one combined OECD mapping.
Define accepted/provisional state fields or caveat conventions sufficient to prevent mixing USD_EXC and USD_PPP outputs.
Define report eligibility policy for exchange-rate current-USD and PPP-basis GDP separately.

Re-run successor check gates preserving proposal existence, auto_apply=false, evidence links, caveat preservation, no conversion/aggregation, proposal immutability, allowed status, report-impact flag, and replay inputs.
Represent any movement as explicit state and manifest deltas before any future base-state or manifest mutation is considered.
recommended_next_action: Create a future, separate review-decision task only if MacroForge is ready to choose basis-specific report eligibility policy; otherwise keep OECD B1GQ deferred with the two basis candidates available for consultation.
